compiled_state.title: Write RED test and add plugin boundary rule to CLAUDE.md Never Do These
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
testing_mode: RED

Approach: Add a new rule (item 26) to the 'Never Do These' numbered list in CLAUDE.md Critical Rules. Create tests/hooks/test-plugin-boundary-rule.sh as the behavioral test.

RED test first (must fail before implementation):
1. Create tests/hooks/test-plugin-boundary-rule.sh (executable) that asserts:
   - CLAUDE.md 'Never Do These' section contains a rule that includes BOTH 'NEVER' AND 'plugins/dso' AND at least one of the permitted dirs (docs/designs/, docs/findings/, docs/archive/, tests/)
   - The NEVER-statement appears on a line before the positive enumeration in the same rule block
   - .test-index has an entry mapping CLAUDE.md to tests/hooks/test-plugin-boundary-rule.sh
2. Run the test — it must FAIL (RED) because the rule does not exist yet
3. Add entry to .test-index: CLAUDE.md:tests/hooks/test-plugin-boundary-rule.sh [test_plugin_boundary_rule_in_claude_md]

GREEN implementation:
4. Add rule 26 to CLAUDE.md Never Do These section:
   'Never place dev-team artifacts inside plugins/dso/. NEVER write design documents, investigation findings, archive files, or other dev-team work to any directory inside plugins/dso/. Dev-team artifacts belong in project-local directories: docs/designs/, docs/findings/, docs/archive/, tests/.'
   NEVER-statement must appear FIRST in the rule (positional bias).
5. Run the test — it must PASS (GREEN)

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] tests/hooks/test-plugin-boundary-rule.sh exists and is executable
  Verify: test -x tests/hooks/test-plugin-boundary-rule.sh
- [ ] CLAUDE.md Never Do These contains a rule with NEVER + plugins/dso + permitted dirs
  Verify: grep -A3 'NEVER.*plugins/dso\|Never.*plugins/dso' CLAUDE.md | grep -q 'docs/designs'
- [ ] NEVER-statement appears before positive enumeration in the rule
  Verify: bash tests/hooks/test-plugin-boundary-rule.sh
- [ ] .test-index has entry for CLAUDE.md
  Verify: grep -q 'CLAUDE.md' .test-index
- [ ] test-plugin-boundary-rule.sh passes
  Verify: bash tests/hooks/test-plugin-boundary-rule.sh; echo "exit: $?"
